{"query":"TSMC Vanguard International Semiconductor VIS share sale block trade May 2026 ownership percentage reduction","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1562,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://pr.tsmc.com/english/news/3314","title":"TSMC to Sell 8.1% of Vanguard International Semiconductor","content":"The proposed share sale will not affect TSMC’s strategic relations with VIS, including outsourcing of interposer production and licensing of GaN technology to VIS.
The proposed share sale is part of TSMC’s plan to focus its resources on core business activities.
In June 2024, TSMC ceased to have representation on VIS’ board of directors.
Upon the completion of this share sale, TSMC expects to reduce its shareholding in VIS to approximately 19%."},{"url":"https://www.tradingview.com/news/invezz:97284dd33094b:0-tsmc-reduces-vis-ownership-after-announcing-massive-share-sale/","title":"TSMC reduces VIS ownership after announcing massive share sale — TradingView News","content":"The proposed transaction would lower TSMC’s holding in Vanguard International Semiconductor, also known as VIS, to around 19% from approximately 27.1% on a fully diluted basis, according to the company.
At current market prices, the 152 million VIS shares are valued at roughly 26.8 billion Taiwan dollars, equivalent to nearly $850 million.
The chipmaker said the planned share sale would not impact its strategic relationship with VIS.
As of May 15, 2026, TSMC shares were trading around $417 to $418 per share.
TSMC has recorded significant gains over the past 12 months, with the stock rising more than 100% during the period.
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company is a Taiwan-based integrated circuit foundry service provider."},{"url":"https://www.digitimes.com/news/a20260515VL215/tsmc-vis-interposer-business-taiwan.html","title":"TSMC to trim Vanguard International stake to 19% in block trade","content":"TSMC to trim Vanguard International stake to 19% in block trade"},{"url":"https://focustaiwan.tw/business/202605150020","title":"TSMC to sell part of VIS stake, maintain strategic ties - Focus Taiwan","content":"(TSMC) announced Friday that it plans to sell up to 152 million shares in Vanguard International Semiconductor Corp.
TSMC said the sale is expected to reduce its holdings in VIS, a leading specialty IC foundry service provider, by 8.1 percentage points from the current 27.1 percent and will be conducted through block trades to financial investment institutions.
The world's largest contract chipmaker said that the share sale plan for VIS will allow the company to focus its resources on core businesses.
TSMC said it has no plans to sell more of its stake in VIS in the near term."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"TSMC Vanguard International Semiconductor VIS share sale block trade May 2026 ownership percentage reduction","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://pr.tsmc.com/english/news/3314","title":"TSMC to Sell 8.1% of Vanguard International Semiconductor","content":"# TSMC to Sell 8.1% of Vanguard International Semiconductor. **HSINCHU, Taiwan, R.O.C., May 15, 2026 –**TSMC (TWSE: 2330, NYSE: TSM) plans to execute the sale of up to 152.0 million common shares of Vanguard International Semiconductor Corporation (“VIS”), or approximately 8.1% of VIS’ fully-diluted paid-in-capital through a block trade to financial institutional investors. The proposed share sale will not affect TSMC’s strategic relations with VIS, including outsourcing of interposer production and licensing of GaN technology to VIS. The proposed share sale is part of TSMC’s plan to focus its resources on core business activities. In June 2024, TSMC ceased to have representation on VIS’ board of directors. TSMC currently holds approximately 27.1% of VIS shares on a fully diluted basis. Upon the completion of this share sale, TSMC expects to reduce its shareholding in VIS to approximately 19%. TSMC has no plan to sell more VIS shares in the foreseeable future. ### TSMC Spokesperson. ### TSMC Deputy Spokesperson.","score":0.9100432,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/TSM/6-k-taiwan-semiconductor-manufacturing-co-ltd-current-report-foreign--758b42b4aeac.html","title":"TSMC to sell 8.1% Vanguard Semiconductor stake | TSM SEC Filing - Form 6-K","content":"**Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSMC)** plans to sell up to 152.0 million common shares of Vanguard International Semiconductor (VIS), representing about 8.1% of VIS’s fully diluted paid-in capital, through a block trade to financial institutional investors. TSMC currently owns approximately 27.1% of VIS on a fully diluted basis and expects its stake to decline to around 19% after the transaction. The company states that this sale will not affect its strategic relationship with VIS, including outsourcing of interposer production and licensing of GaN technology, and describes the move as part of a broader plan to concentrate resources on core business activities. VIS shares to be sold: 152.0 million shares Portion of VIS capital: 8.1% Current VIS ownership: 27.1% +4 more.
